overall.description: A first-person view from a ship's bridge at night, navigating through a coastal waterway toward harbor. The scene features three operational navigation displays (chart plotter, radar, and depth sounder) mounted above the forward windows, with the vessel's bow visible ahead. A brief lightning flash illuminates the deck during the sequence, indicating storm conditions. The footage appears to be from a maritime simulation or training software.

scene_state.functional_zone: ship bridge navigating coastal channel at night

events.description: distant navigation lights become visible on the horizon ahead
bright lightning flash illuminates the ship deck and ocean surface temporarily

agents.ego_description: operating the ship's helm and monitoring navigation displays while proceeding through the channel

entered.detail: chart plotter display showing the vessel's position in a narrow channel with landmasses on either side
circular radar display with rotating sweep showing surrounding targets
depth sounder display showing water column profile
the bow deck and railings of the vessel visible through the forward windows
dark ocean surface extending to the horizon

segments.scene_description: The view from a ship's bridge at night, with three navigation displays mounted overhead showing chart, radar, and depth data. The vessel moves steadily through a coastal channel, with distant navigation lights appearing on the horizon. At approximately six seconds, a bright lightning flash temporarily illuminates the deck and water surface, revealing storm conditions outside.

events.description: red and green channel marker lights become visible on the right side

agents.ego_description: continuing navigation toward the harbor entrance while monitoring the radar for nearby traffic

entered.detail: red and green navigation buoys marking the channel entrance

segments.scene_description: Continued navigation from the ship's bridge as the vessel approaches the harbor entrance. Channel marker lights appear on the starboard side, guiding the approach. The radar continues its rotation, completing nearly a full sweep as the ship maintains steady progress through the dark waters.

merged_segments.scene_description: The shot follows a continuous first-person perspective from a ship's bridge as it navigates a dark coastal channel toward the lights of a harbor entrance. It begins with a view of three overhead navigation displays showing chart, radar, and depth data while the vessel moves steadily toward distant horizon lights. As the ship progresses, a sudden, bright flash of lightning illuminates the deck and the surrounding water, momentarily revealing the intensity of the storm conditions. The scene then shifts toward the harbor approach, where channel marker lights emerge on the starboard side to guide the vessel. Finally, the camera maintains its forward momentum through the dark waters as the radar completes its rotation, signaling the ship's steady arrival at the port.
